caption.line2:
A sculptural low stool
 made in Portugal from cork.

home.intro:
YOMARTINS is the first object by Andrea Dea Jones.
 A compact object with weight, softness and presence.
home.copyright: © Andrea Dea Jones. All rights reserved.

objects.intro: Each object is developed independently, yet part of the same material and conceptual language where sustainability and elegance naturally coexist.

collections.introLine1: Each collection brings together a family of objects.
collections.introLine2: Connected by a shared language of material, structure, and presence.

yomartins.description: A collection shaped by cork, structure, and presence.

atelierStudies.description: A testing collection built from repeated object studies for layout, routing, and card behavior.

editions.title: Editions

objectEdition.title: Objects Edition
objectEdition.description: Our objects exist in different editions. Each edition defines how an object is produced, customized, and distributed, without ever compromising its conceptual integrity.

studio.title: Studio Edition
studio.description: The Studio Edition is produced to order. It allows for variations in materials and finishes, while respecting the object's original form and intent.

gallery.title: Gallery Edition
gallery.description: The Gallery Edition is produced in limited series. Each version is defined by a precise combination of materials and finishes, chosen as a specific interpretation of the object.

editions.note: Not all editions are available for every object. Some pieces exist only as Studio Editions, others only as Gallery Editions.

contact.title: GET IN TOUCH
contact.description: For inquiries, commissions or collaborations, please use the form below or contact directly. All messages are answered personally.
contact.name: Name

about.portraitAlt: RACHEL CAMBIER, founder of Andrea Dea Jones

paragraphs.p1: Andrea Dea Jones is a design practice centered on objects, material, and form.
paragraphs.p2: Created by Rachel Cambier, it brings together an interior architect’s eye with a growing body of objects shaped by structure, presence, and character.
paragraphs.p3: Trained in interior architecture in Belgium, Rachel developed her practice through work across Belgium, France, and Portugal, moving between interiors, bespoke design, and object-making.
paragraphs.p4: Now based in Portugal, she works across spaces and objects, with a particular interest in pieces that feel grounded, tactile, and quietly expressive.
paragraphs.p5: Rachel Cambier is also co-founder of JORA Studio, an architecture and interior design practice based in the Algarve.
